data.body: PASS 1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification): PASS, no findings. The T1 bright-line fires (new REVIEW_RESULT event type as an observability substrate), but the plan explicitly grounds itself in established in-repo prior art rather than inventing a novel architecture: it reuses the existing reducer-ignored sidecar pattern (REVIEW_RESULT is already in docs/reuse-surface.md and _NON_REPLAY_KNOWN_TYPES / docs/event-schema.md), "reuse[s] rebar's existing event compaction" for retention, and the rollout AC follows "rebar's preserved-and-ignored-by-older-clones pattern" (the documented forward-compat mechanism in docs/event-schema.md and tests/interfaces/contracts/test_event_schema_forward_compat.py). No unverified external/library capability assertions. Anti-repackaging and prior-art checks satisfied.
